ccel.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

title: Concerning the Heaven.

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The heaven is the circumference of things created, both visible and invisible.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For within its boundary are included and marked off both the mental faculties of the angels and all the world of sense.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But the Deity alone is uncircumscribed, filling all things, and surrounding all things, and bounding all things, for He is above all things, and has created all things.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Since, therefore, the Scripture speaks of heaven, and heaven of heaven, and heavens of heavens, and the blessed Paul says that he was snatched away to the third heaven, we say that in the cosmogony of the universe we accept the creation of a heaven which the foreign philosophers, appropriating the views of Moses, call a starless sphere.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But further, God called the firmament also heaven, which He commanded to be in the midst of the waters, setting it to divide the waters that are above the firmament from the waters that are below the firmament.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And its nature, according to the divine Basilius, who is versed in the mysteries of divine Scripture, is delicate as smoke.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Others, however, hold that it is watery in nature, since it is set in the midst of the waters: others say it is composed of the four elements: and lastly, others speak of it as a fifth body, distinct from the four elements.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Further, some have thought that the heaven encircles the universe and has the form of a sphere, and that everywhere it is the highest point, and that the centre of the space enclosed by it is the lowest part: and, further, that those bodies that are light and airy are allotted by the Creator the upper region: while those that are heavy and tend to descend occupy the lower region, which is the middle.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The element, then, that is lightest and most inclined to soar upwards is fire, and hence they hold that its position is immediately after the heaven, and they call it ether, and after it comes the lower air.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But earth and water, which are heavier and have more of a downward tendency, are suspended in the centre.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Therefore, taking them in the reverse order, we have in the lowest situation earth and water: but water is lighter than earth, and hence is more easily set in motion: above these on all hands, like a covering, is the circle of air, and all round the air is the circle of ether, and outside air is the circle of the heaven.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Further, they say that the heaven moves in a circle and so compresses all that is within it, that they remain firm and not liable to fall asunder.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: They say also that there are seven zones of the heaven, one higher than the other.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And its nature, they say, is of extreme fineness, like that of smoke, and each zone contains one of the planets.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For there are said to be seven planets: Sol, Luna, Jupiter, Mercury, Mars, Venus and Saturn.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But sometimes Venus is called Lucifer and sometimes Vesper.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: These are called planets because their movements are the reverse of those of the heaven.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For while the heaven and all other stars move from east to west, these alone move from west to east.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And this can easily be seen in the case of the moon, which moves each evening a little backwards.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: All, therefore, who hold that the heaven is in the form of a sphere, say that it is equally removed and distant from the earth at all points, whether above, or sideways, or below.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And by ‘below’ and ‘sideways’ I mean all that comes within the range of our senses.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For it follows from what has been said, that the heaven occupies the whole of the upper region and the earth the whole of the lower.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: They say, besides, that the heaven encircles the earth in the manner of a sphere, and bears along with it in its most rapid revolutions sun, moon and stars, and that when the sun is over the earth it becomes day there, and when it is under the earth it is night.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And, again, when the sun goes under the earth it is night here, but day yonder.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Others have pictured the heaven as a hemisphere.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: This idea is suggested by these words of David, the singer of God, Who stretchest out the heavens like a curtain, by which word he clearly means a tent: and by these from the blessed Isaiah, Who hath established the heavens like a vault: and also because when the sun, moon, and stars set they make a circuit round the earth from west to north, and so reach once more the east.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Still, whether it is this way or that, all things have been made and established by the divine command, and have the divine will and counsel for a foundation that cannot be moved.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For He Himself spoke and they were made: He Himself commanded and they were created.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: He hath also established them for ever and ever: He hath made a decree which will not pass.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The heaven of heaven, then, is the first heaven which is above the firmament.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: So here we have two heavens, for God called the firmament also Heaven.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And it is customary in the divine Scripture to speak of the air also as heavens, because we see it above us.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Bless Him, it says, all ye birds of the heaven, meaning of the air.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For it is the air and not the heaven that is the region in which birds fly.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: So here we have three heavens, as the divine Apostle said.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But if you should wish to look upon the seven zones as seven heavens there is no injury done to the word of truth.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For it is usual in the Hebrew tongue to speak of heaven in the plural, that is, as heavens, and when a Hebrew wishes to say heaven of heaven, he usually says heavens of heavens, and this clearly means heaven of heaven, which is above the firmament, and the waters which are above the heavens, whether it is the air and the firmament, or the seven zones of the firmament, or the firmament itself which are spoken of in the plural as heavens according to the Hebrew custom.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: All things, then, which are brought into existence are subject to corruption according to the law of their nature, and so even the heavens themselves are corruptible.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But by the grace of God they are maintained and preserved.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Only the Deity, however, is by nature without beginning and without end.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Wherefore it has been said, They will perish, but Thou dost endure: nevertheless, the heavens will not be utterly destroyed.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For they will wax old and be wound round as a covering, and will be changed, and there will be a new heaven and a new earth.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the great part the heaven is greater than the earth, but we need not investigate the essence of the heaven, for it is quite beyond our knowledge.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It must not be supposed that the heavens or the luminaries are endowed with life.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For they are inanimate and insensible.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: So that when the divine Scripture saith, Let the heavens rejoice and the earth be glad, it is the angels in heaven and the men on earth that are invited to rejoice.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the Scripture is familiar with the figure of personification, and is wont to speak of inanimate things as though they were animate: for example, The sea saw it and fled: Jordan was driven back.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And again, What ailed thee, O thou sea, that thou fleddest? thou, O Jordan, that thou was driven back?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Mountains, too, and hills are asked the reason of their leaping in the same way as we are wont to say, the city was gathered together, when we do not mean the buildings, but the inhabitants of the city: again, the heavens declare the glory of God, does not mean that they send forth a voice that can be heard by bodily ears, but that from their own greatness they bring before our minds the power of the Creator: and when we contemplate their beauty we praise the Maker as the Master-Craftsman.
